LG SL10YG is a high-end soundbar priced at $1,000, featuring a wireless subwoofer, Dolby Atmos and DTS:X support, and excellent sound quality.

With a budget of $300-$400 and a focus on good sound quality and a wireless subwoofer, the Sonos Beam with Wireless Subwoofer is recommended. The Sonos Beam is a compact soundbar known for its excellent sound quality, and when paired with the Sonos Wireless Subwoofer, it becomes a powerful system. Together, they can be obtained for around $350-$400 during sales or discounts.

The Samsung HW-Q60R is another recommended option, offering better sound quality and a more immersive experience compared to the HW-Q50R. It has a wireless subwoofer, supports Dolby Atmos and DTS:X, and is usually priced around $350-$400.
The Yamaha YAS-209BL is a popular choice in the $300-$350 price range, providing good sound quality, a wireless subwoofer, and built-in Alexa support.
The Polk Command Bar is a great option for a system with a wireless subwoofer and good sound quality, also featuring built-in Alexa support and is usually priced around $300-$350.
The JBL Bar 3.1 offers good sound quality, a wireless subwoofer, and a sleek design, and is usually priced around $300-$350.

All the recommended soundbars (Sonos Beam with Wireless Subwoofer, Samsung HW-Q60R, Yamaha YAS-209BL, Polk Command Bar, JBL Bar 3.1) should provide good sound quality and a wireless subwoofer, enhancing the movie-watching experience in a medium-sized room.

The Sonos Wireless Subwoofer (also known as the Sonos Sub) is a compact, wireless subwoofer designed to add deep bass and depth to your music and movie experience. It connects wirelessly to the Sonos Beam using Sonos' proprietary wireless technology, making setup seamless and cable-free.

To set up the Sonos Sub, first ensure the Sonos Beam is plugged in and connected to your WiFi network. Open the Sonos app, follow the prompts to add the Sub, place it in a corner of the room (about 6-8 feet away from the Beam), and plug it in. The Sonos app guides you through a calibration process to optimize performance.

The Sonos Sub produces deep bass frequencies as low as 25 Hz, enhancing the overall audio experience without cluttering space due to its compact and sleek design. It is compatible with all Sonos speakers, including the Beam.

Users can experiment with different placements for the Sonos Sub to find the optimal spot in the room. Adjusting the Sub's volume and settings via the Sonos app allows fine-tuning the sound to personal preference.

The Sonos app is primarily designed to control and customize Sonos speakers, including the Beam and Sub. However, Sonos has introduced some TV control features in recent years, but they are limited and dependent on your TV's compatibility.

Sonos has partnered with certain TV manufacturers to allow for basic TV control through the Sonos app. This feature is called "TV Control" or "TV Audio Control." With compatible TVs, you can adjust the TV's volume and mute or unmute the TV's audio using the Sonos app.

This TV control feature is limited to specific TV brands and models. Currently, Sonos supports TV control for Samsung TVs (2019 or later models), LG TVs (2019 or later models), and Sony TVs (2020 or later models).

If your TV is not compatible with Sonos' TV Control feature, you can still use the Sonos app to control your TV's volume and mute functions using the TV's remote control. This is done through a feature called "IR Control" or "TV Remote Control." The Sonos Beam can learn the TV's remote control commands.

The Sonos app does not currently allow you to switch the audio input on your TV, such as from HDMI 1 to HDMI 2. To switch the audio input on your TV, you'll need to use your TV's remote control and navigate to the input selection menu.

The Sonos app is primarily designed to control your Sonos speakers and their settings, as well as provide a convenient way to play music and audio content. It can control the TV's volume and mute functions on compatible TVs, but it does not have the capability to switch the TV's input sources.

If you have a smart TV with HDMI-CEC (Consumer Electronics Control) support, you might be able to use the Sonos app to switch the TV's input source using HDMI-CEC. However, this requires specific setup and configuration on both your TV and Sonos device, and it's not a standard feature across all Sonos devices or TVs.
The Sonos app provides sound customization options for the Beam, including adjusting bass, treble, and midrange levels through an equalization (EQ) interface with sliders.
Sonos offers pre-set sound modes for the Beam such as TV Mode, Music Mode, and Night Mode, which are optimized for different listening scenarios.
The Beam can be configured with a Speech Enhancement feature via the Sonos app to improve clarity of dialogue and vocals, adjustable to user preference.
Bass adjustment on the Beam can be set from -3 to +3 using the Sonos app.
The Sonos app allows users to control the Loudness feature on the Beam, which adjusts overall volume and compression with settings of Off, Low, or High.
Users can create and save up to three custom sound profiles for the Beam in the Sonos app, tailored for different types of content like movies, music, or podcasts.

The project involved writing a book that covers various philosophical and psychological concepts such as emotional intelligence, self-awareness, the power of habits, self-care, and mindfulness, and their impact on an individual's overall well-being. The book also includes a bonus chapter, which is a fictional story illustrating the application of these concepts in everyday life.

Throughout the project, various research and scientific studies were incorporated to support the arguments and ideas presented in the book. The Harvard referencing method was used to cite the sources used in the book. Additionally, links to free images that relate to the topics discussed in the book were provided.

The key takeaways from this project include the importance of constantly evaluating one's beliefs and experiences to gain a deeper understanding of oneself and the world around them, the impact of emotional intelligence on relationships, and the power of mindfulness in promoting mental health.
